title: Creamy Tomato Tortellini Soup
description: This comforting and quick soup is perfect for a family meal, featuring tender tortellini in a rich, creamy tomato broth. It's designed to be kid-approved and easy to make with minimal cleanup.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Stir in heavy cream and tortellini. Cook according to package directions, usually 3-5 minutes for fresh tortellini or 6-8 minutes for frozen, until tender.
Stir in baby spinach until wilted, about 1-2 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot, garnished with grated Parmesan cheese.
tips: For extra hidden veggies, blend a cooked carrot or sweet potato into the soup base before adding cream and tortellini.
If you prefer a thicker soup, mash some of the tortellini against the side of the pot after cooking.
Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop.
